description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:18 Docker image with a cache for node_modules/ and .yarn. Define two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add an update_stops_data job that runs the before_script commands `corepack enable` and `yarn install --frozen-lockfile`, then installs openssh-client if missing, starts ssh-agent, adds the `$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY`, creates and secures `~/.ssh`, adds gitlab.com to known hosts, configures git user email and name, and only runs on schedules or web triggers. Its script executes `yarn run update-stops`, `git remote set-url origin git@gitlab.com:omgtransit/omgtransit.git`, `git checkout $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME`, `git add src/static/transit_stops.json`, and a conditional block: `if git diff --staged --quiet; then echo "No changes to stops data"; else COMMIT_DATE=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d'); git commit -m "Update stops data $COMMIT_DATE (CI) [skip ci]"; git push origin $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME; fi`. In the deploy stage add a pages job that also runs `corepack enable` and `yarn install --frozen-lockfile`, then runs `yarn run build`, removes any existing `public` directory, moves the `build` output to `public`, and publishes the `public/` folder as a GitLab Pages artifact; this job only runs on the main branch.
